author: Zachary Milot
description: ArrayTable is a utility library that treats a 2D array as a lightweight “table” abstraction with optional column names, providing a consistent, 1-based API for building tables, reading and updating cells by index or column name, adding/removing rows and columns, searching for matching rows, performing common transformations (such as sorting, swapping, and transposing), and importing/exporting data as CSV, with bundled examples and generated reference documentation.

versions.author: Zachary Milot

author: Corsin Alig, Bernd Huf, Stephan Gieriet
description: Provides configurable multi-level trace logging and diagnostic output for variables, arrays, sequences, and function execution flow to support method development, debugging, and validation.

author: Oliver Funke
description: The HSLExtensions Framework library is the common foundation for the Hamilton VENUS HSL extension set: it defines shared enumerations and core type metadata, provides runtime type-check helpers for scalars and arrays, implements safe conversion utilities between string, integer, float, and boolean values (including success/failure reporting and normalization like float string cleanup), and centralizes tracing/error logging with configurable trace levels for debug, warning, and parameter diagnostics; together, it gives all higher-level extension libraries a consistent base for validation, conversion, and troubleshooting so they behave predictably across the whole package.
